title: Is This The Extended OP
wikitext:
[]: Is This The Extended OP?             Quote Post by menlospree » Tue Feb 02, 2010 3:51 am I recently found this video on YouTube titled "Neon Genesis Evangelion OP (Full)". I've heard next to nothing about it, and I noticed that a few people posted comments saying that it isn't legit (but of course, YouTube commentors are hardly reliable). So is this video the real deal or just something a fan made? Video here

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Feb 02, 2010 4:26 am it's fan made, trust me there was never an alternative open of any kind used it has been alleged on the liner notes of the soundtrack that Anno had originally wanted to use Borodin's "Polovtsian Dances" as the opening, but the network thought the audience would sense the oddness that Evangelion would be eventually known for with a classical/opera-type song as the theme, and the J-pop "Cruel Angel's Thesis" was used as a compromise I wish the former had been used though, it would have been far more fitting in so many ways and is also infinitely more pretty for more: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Polovetsian_Dances

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Tue Feb 02, 2010 8:39 am Huh? Yes, that is full OP. There was an official full opening included on renewal set or something. A fan did not get these images out of nowhere, as they appear in that vid. No way in hell a video this well made is by a fan.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Feb 02, 2010 9:30 am THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: it's fan made, trust me there was never an alternative open of any kind used

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Feb 02, 2010 10:28 am THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: it's fan made, trust me there was never an alternative open of any kind used

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Tue Feb 02, 2010 2:13 pm Monkey, those are some interesting images. The one is of Eva 01's core and head correct?

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed Feb 03, 2010 1:56 am The Eva Monkey wrote: It's official. You can find it on this DVD: http://www.cdjapan.co.jp/detailview.html?KEY=KIBA-1000 I've had it on Eva Monkey for ages: http://www.evamonkey.com/test_type_opening.avi Images: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Category:Images_%28Full_Opening_Screenshots%29 See also: Test-Type DVD http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=4861

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Feb 03, 2010 3:34 am ...who said it was ever aired on tv?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Feb 03, 2010 8:44 am THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: what was(/were) the original air date(s) then?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Feb 03, 2010 11:00 am Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: Monkey, those are some interesting images. The one is of Eva 01's core and head correct?

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Wed Feb 03, 2010 1:14 pm Shin-seiki wrote: Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: Monkey, those are some interesting images. The one is of Eva 01's core and head correct?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Feb 04, 2010 8:42 am tomrule123 wrote: It was on the laserdisc and then it was removed on Renewal, but then placed on the opening. Wonder why they did that.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Feb 04, 2010 2:55 pm Shin-seiki wrote: Never heard of any official reason, but it was probably because the Reiquarium versions were so hard to see, or at least make out what the heck they were supposed to be.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Feb 27, 2010 3:12 pm Reichu wrote: Shin-seiki wrote: Never heard of any official reason, but it was probably because the Reiquarium versions were so hard to see, or at least make out what the heck they were supposed to be.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Feb 27, 2010 5:57 pm Reichu wrote: They could have had the clean images flash on the screen briefly (like the ones during Misato's expositional speech in EoE) instead of excising them.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Feb 27, 2010 6:30 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Reichu wrote: They could have had the clean images flash on the screen briefly (like the ones during Misato's expositional speech in EoE) instead of excising them.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Sat Feb 27, 2010 6:41 pm Ah, that video pretty much summed up everything that made NGE great. I'm getting nostalgic...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Feb 27, 2010 8:03 pm tomrule123 wrote: I agree. In EoE, we couldn't recall what was actually in the images during the Instrumentality Project. That needed frame-by-frame observations.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Feb 27, 2010 11:12 pm Reichu wrote: tomrule123 wrote: I agree. In EoE, we couldn't recall what was actually in the images during the Instrumentality Project. That needed frame-by-frame observations.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Sat Feb 27, 2010 11:17 pm Ha, never seen this before. Got to say I really love it though

I've heard next to nothing about it, and I noticed that a few people posted comments saying that it isn't legit (but of course, YouTube commentors are hardly reliable).
